items.title: Queen departs for Uzbekistan to attend international Islamic civilization conference
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, has departed for Tashkent, Uzbekistan today to attend an international conference on Islamic civilisation. The Queen will participate in the conference titled "Scientific Tradition in Islamic Civilisation: Sources, Potentials and Challenges", scheduled to take place at the Center of Islamic Civilisation in Tashkent. Earlier today, Her Majesty departed for Uzbekistan from the Royal Hangar at Senai International Airport.

items.title: Malaysians warned over dubious 'badal haj' offers
items.summary: JEDDAH: Malaysians are advised not to be fooled by dubious offers of badal haj (haj by proxy) services, especially those offered at "extraordinarily" low prices compared to the actual cost of the pilgrimage. Malaysian Consul General in Jeddah Tengku Mohd Dzaraif Raja Abdul Kadir said the cost of performing haj is high, hence any unreasonable offer is feared to not meet the prescribed pillars and obligatory haj. Mohd Dzaraif explained that for domestic haj packages in Saudi Arabia itself, the minimum cost is around RM9,000 to RM10,000.

items.title: MCMC warns against sharing provocative 3R content online
items.summary: CYBERJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission has issued a stern warning to the public against producing or distributing online content touching on race, religion, and the royal institution (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred, or risks triggering public tension. "The MCMC takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could harm social harmony. The commission said it would work with the police and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate, and take action against content that violated the law.

items.title: MCMC, police to act on provocative 3R material
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The public has been reminded not to produce or disseminate content related to race, religion and royalty (3R), particularly material that is provocative, insulting, incites hatred or could trigger public tension. In a statement today, the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) said it takes seriously the misuse of network services and online platforms that could undermine social harmony. MCMC also said it will work closely with the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) and relevant agencies to monitor, investigate and take action against content that violates the law.

items.title: Health Ministry says 56 heat-related illnesses, two deaths reported since January
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 3 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has recorded 56 cases related to hot weather nationwide, including two deaths from heat stroke, from Jan 1 to today. According to the statement, there were 47 cases involved heat exhaustion, four cases of exertional heat stroke, four cases of heat stroke and one case of heat cramps. MOH said analysis showed that 58 per cent of cases of heat-related illnesses were related to physical activity during hot weather.

items.title: Man dies 2 days after Johor crash, buried beside wife and daughter
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: A Proton Persona driver involved in a crash at Km71, Jalan Johor Baru–Mersing on Friday died last night. Mohd Aiman, 20, the third child of Kamilin Alias, 56, and Rubeta Mohd, 51, confirmed the death when contacted yesterday. He said his father (Kamilin) was pronounced dead at 10.35pm.

items.title: Missing Bandar Mahkota Cheras girl found dead in Sungai Langat after three-day search
items.summary: KAJANG, May 3 — The body of nine-year-old girl Nur Qaseh Deandra Mohd Qayyum, who was reported missing after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, has been found in Sungai Langat, ending a three-day search. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Operations Assistant Director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar confirmed the discovery, Harian Metro reported this afternoon. Harian Metro also reported that Nur Qaseh had identified her body at the scene.

items.title: Mental health at work to become legal duty for employers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) is moving to make mental health protection a statutory requirement for employers, framing psychosocial wellbeing as a legal duty under the Occupational Safety and Health Act. The push to strengthen mental health protection forms part of the Occupational Safety and Health Master Plan 2026–2030 (OSHMP30), which the department began implementing earlier this year. Strategic Thrust 2 of the plan focuses on enhancing worker health and wellbeing, including mental health promotion, occupational health management, and the creation of workplaces that are safe from both physical and psychosocial hazards.

items.title: Evolving landscape of modern diplomacy
items.summary: IN an era marked by great-power rivalry, technological disruption and growing global uncertainty, a fundamental question arises: does diplomacy still matter? In this context, diplomacy is not simply relevant. Diplomacy enables states to translate national interests into international outcomes through dialogue, negotiation, and cooperation.

items.title: Crowded court as 113 foreigners charged with illegal entry, overstaying
items.summary: PASIR MAS: It was a packed day at the Pasir Mas Sessions Court as 113 undocumented migrants were charged with immigration offences. All 113, charged either with illegal entry or overstaying, pleaded guilty before Sessions Court judge Zulkifli Abdullah. Of the total, 85 were charged under Section 15(1)(c) of the Immigration Act for overstaying, while the remaining 28 were charged under Section 6(1)(c) for entering the country without valid travel documents.

items.title: Anwar orders Mara Act amendments to be completed within three months
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed that the draft amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act be completed within three months. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the move includes streamlining Mara’s role as a key umbrella for the Bumiputera economy as well as a one-stop centre for development through better coordination of related agencies and policies. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki and Mara director-general Datuk Zulfikri Osman.

items.title: EAIC flags high-risk gaps in fuel subsidy crisis management
items.summary: Its chairman Tan Sri Dr Ismail Bakar said the risks increased when enforcement is carried out under urgent conditions to prevent leakages of public resources, including subsidies for RON95 petrol, diesel and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). He said that based on EAIC's oversight of 20 enforcement agencies, three main risk areas had been identified during crisis periods – including the smuggling of subsidised fuel, particularly at border points where enforcement officers may abuse their powers or fail to adhere to standard operating procedures (SOPs). Ismail said EAIC had also flagged risks of misappropriation of fuel supplies among enforcement personnel, particularly where operational allocations are diverted for personal use or benefit.

items.title: Fadhlina orders review of SPM Moral Education subject after complaints over difficulty
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has directed the Malaysian Examinations Syndicate to review and improve the Moral Education subject following complaints from the Indian community. "When the subject of Moral Education was raised, and given the anxieties of parents and the community, I have already directed the Examinations Syndicate to review and improve the Moral Education subject," she said. The issue was raised by Surain ahead of her address, and Fadhlina said she was grateful for the community's candour.

items.title: Indonesia's Mount Semeru erupts, ash column reaches 900 metres
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia's Mount Semeru on the border of Lumajang and Malang regencies in East Java erupted Sunday morning, sending an ash column up to 900 metres above its summit, authorities said. In a statement, an observation officer at the Semeru monitoring post Sigit Rian Alfian said the eruption occurred at 8am local time, with the ash plume reaching about 4,576 metres above sea level. Authorities maintained Semeru's alert status at Level III (Alert) and warned residents against all activities within 13 kilometres of the summit in the southeast sector.

items.title: Social security for Malaysians abroad: Bill to be tabled at next Parliament sitting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will table amendments to the Self-Employment Social Security Act at the next Parliament sitting to expand coverage for Malaysians working abroad. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the move, which begins with cross-border workers in Singapore, has received cabinet approval. Ramanan said the amendments are crucial to addressing workplace safety and health.

items.title: North to south, west to east: MetMalaysia warns of stormy weather nationwide, including Klang Valley
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect the capital and eight other states until 3 pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). The department also forecast similar weather conditions across Kuala Lumpur and parts of Selangor, namely Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. Similar conditions are also expected in Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah and Rembau) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru), as well as in Sabah involving Tawau (Lahad Datu), Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat (Pitas and Kudat).

items.title: MyDigital ID SSO rollout for MyJPJ smooth, no glitches reported
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The rollout of the MyDigital ID Single Sign-On (SSO) integration for the MyJPJ application, enforced Friday (May 1), has proceeded smoothly with no technical disruptions reported. MyDigital ID said in a statement that the system remained stable and is functioning as intended, enabling users to access services more easily and securely. To date, some seven million MyJPJ users have registered and logged in using MyDigital ID, bringing the total number of MyDigital ID users to 12 million.

items.title: [UPDATED] Body of 9-year-old swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras found near Kajang
items.summary: The body was discovered by search and rescue teams sweeping the Sungai Langat river near Kajang town. The discovery was confirmed by Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Zone Four chief Wan Syariman Wan Salleh. "The body was found by the Fire and Rescue Department's Water Rescue Team (PPDA), who conducted a search by boat along the river," he said.

items.title: Heatwave toll: 56 cases, 2 dead despite 'low-risk' conditions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry has recorded 56 cases of heat-related illnesses nationwide, including two deaths from heatstroke, between Jan 1 and today. Of the total cases, 47 were heat exhaustion, four exertional heatstroke, four heatstroke and one heat cramp. The ministry said its analysis found that 58 per cent of heat-related cases were linked to physical activity during hot weather.

items.title: Adverse weather expected in KL, 8 states until 3pm — MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to affect the capital and eight other states until 3 pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). The department also forecast similar weather conditions across Kuala Lumpur and parts of Selangor, namely Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang. Similar conditions are also expected in Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Seremban, Kuala Pilah and Rembau) and Johor (Tangkak, Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru), as well as in Sabah involving Tawau (Lahad Datu), Sandakan (Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan) and Kudat (Pitas and Kudat).

items.title: Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain tragedy: Recovered ball renews hope in search for missing 9-year-old
items.summary: The girl's aunt, Fakhira Fatin Mohd Saad, 31, said the ball has been recovered and has given the family renewed hope as the search and rescue (SAR) operation entered its third day today. "Everyone in the family hopes Qaseh will be found today. Our only hope is for Qaseh to be found soon," she said.

items.title: More Nepalis drive electric, evading global fuel shocks
items.summary: KATHMANDU: As global fuel markets reel from the Middle East war, motorists in Nepal are increasingly turning to electric vehicles, with high demand putting a strain on dealerships. "My profession is not affected (by the conflict)," said the 48-year-old, who drives his 18-seater Chinese-made e-van along Nepal's highways daily. "One of the main reasons is the increased penetration of electric vehicles on Nepal's roads," alternative energy expert Govind Raj Pokharel told AFP.

items.title: Visit Chinese, Tamil schools too, Fadhlina tells education officials
items.summary: "Chinese children are our children. SJKCs (Chinese schools) are our schools. SJKTs (Tamil schools) are our schools."

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: Bumiputera-empowerment agenda bolder than ever under Madani govt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has defended the Madani government's Bumiputera empowerment agenda, saying it has been pursued more "assertively, firmly and boldly" than before. Dismissing claims by certain quarters, Anwar said his administration has not supported elites at the expense of Bumiputera interests. "Previously, matriculation was 100 per cent reserved for Bumiputera students, but 10 per cent has since been allocated, particularly for underprivileged and marginalised non-Malay students.

items.title: Vietnam January-April coffee exports rise 16pct to 810,000 tonnes
items.summary: HANOI: Vietnam exported 810,000 metric tonnes of coffee in the first four months of 2026, up 15.8 per cent from the same period a year earlier, government data showed on Sunday. Export revenue over the period fell 7 per cent to US$3.69 billion, the statistics office said in a report. In April alone, Vietnam exported 220,000 tonnes of beans, with a value of US$936 million.

items.title: Early morning blaze guts five cars at George Town flat
items.summary: Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant operations director John Sagun Francis said his department received a call about the fire at 4.30 am and a fire brigade team from the Jalan Perak Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) immediately went to the location. “Upon arrival, the fire department found that the fire involved five vehicles in the ground floor parking area of the flat and it also involved part of the garage with an estimated 50 per cent damage. “No casualties were reported in the incident and the fire department managed to control the fire at 4.38 am before the fire was completely extinguished at 5.09 am,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Anwar wants Mara to be Bumiputera development one-stop centre
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) should serve as a central economic umbrella and a one-stop centre for Bumiputera development. He said under this expanded role, Mara would be entrusted with coordinating Bumiputera development efforts across all relevant agencies in the country. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Deputy Rural and Regional Development Minister Datuk Rubiah Wang, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, and Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki.

items.title: #NSTviral: Car vaults over divider, crashes into oncoming traffic [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A car lost control and vaulted across a road divider , crashing into two oncoming vehicles in a high-speed collision captured on dashcam. The video begins with a quiet road before a car from the opposite direction suddenly hurtles over the median, slamming directly into an oncoming vehicle in the right lane. The force of the impact sent the car airborne, where it struck a second vehicle following closely behind from where the dashcam was filming.

items.title: Zahid sets year-end deadline for legal safeguards on Mara’s RM23b assets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Amendments to the Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) Act 1966 will serve as a legal safeguard to ensure the Bumiputera empowerment agenda remains intact and is not altered with every change in national or organisational leadership. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the move is aimed at establishing a stronger governance framework and stricter regulations to ensure the agency’s direction remains consistent in upholding Bumiputera interests, free from political influence. “We do not want Mara’s agenda to change simply because of a change in leadership.

items.title: Water Festival boosts SME activity amid tough operating climate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Water Festival Bukit Bintang 2026 has delivered meaningful economic spillovers for small and medium enterprises (SMEs), reinforcing the role of large-scale tourism events as catalysts for business activity amid a challenging operating environment. In a statement, SME Association of Malaysia president Dr Chin Chee Seong said large-scale, vibrant tourism events such as this are more than cultural celebrations, describing them as important economic catalysts at a time when SMEs are facing slower growth, rising costs and an uncertain business environment. Chin said Bukit Bintang, as a key tourism and commercial hub, provides a strategic platform to highlight Malaysia's diversity, creativity and hospitality.

items.title: Latecomer Japan eyes slice of rising global defence spending
items.summary: TOKYO: Long-pacifist Japan has shed its self-imposed ban on lethal weaponry exports, gunning for a prominent place in the global defence trade – a challenging feat that analysts say could take years to achieve. Five Japanese firms, including Mitsubishi Heavy Industries and Kawasaki Heavy Industries, are already in the top 100 global defence companies, according to the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI). But they have largely lived off domestic demand from Japan's military, often in cooperation with US defence firms.

items.title: Honda Accord driver detained over dangerous overtaking video
items.summary: PONTIAN: Police have detained the driver of a Honda Accord who went viral for dangerous driving on Friday after overtaking several vehicles along a double line. District police chief Superintendent Hadzrat Hussein Mion Hussain said authorities identified the driver based on information received. "Initial investigations found the suspect admitted to driving a silver Honda Accord along a double line and overtaking several vehicles at the location," he said.

items.title: Anwar: No elitism in Madani’s Bumiputera empowerment approach
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The Bumiputera empowerment agenda has been pursued more assertively, firmly and boldly under the Madani Government over the past three years, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar, who is also finance minister, said the approach demonstrates that the government does not subscribe to elitism at the expense of Bumiputera interests, as alleged by certain quarters. “At the same time, 90 per cent of Bumiputera students continue to be given opportunities through the matriculation programme, reflecting the government’s inclusive approach.

items.title: Malaysia key EU partner, ties on upward trajectory
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is a key partner to the European Union (EU), with relations on an upward trajectory, driven by ongoing negotiations on the Malaysia-EU Free Trade Agreement (MEUFTA), said the EU Ambassador to Malaysia, Rafael Daerr. "Our economies are strongly interconnected and, judging by statistics and the positive trade balance, the potential for increase is comparatively higher with the EU than with other countries or regions in the world, alongside the similarly underdeveloped intra-Asean trade," he said, noting that Malaysia is currently EU's 20th-biggest goods trading partner. Drawing similarities between Malaysia and EU member states in terms of population and economic size, the ambassador highlighted the importance of working together on the world stage.

items.title: #NSTviral: Car vaults over divider, crashes into oncoming traffic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A car lost control and vaulted across a road divider, crashing into two oncoming vehicles in a high-speed collision captured on dashcam. The video begins with a quiet road before a car from the opposite direction suddenly hurtles over the median, slamming directly into an oncoming vehicle in the right lane. The force of the impact sent the car airborne, where it struck a second vehicle following closely behind from where the dashcam was filming.

items.title: North Korea calls US cyber crime accusations 'absurd slander'
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea dismissed on Sunday US accusations that it has engaged in cyber crimes to generate illicit revenues, calling the criticism "absurd slander." The US Justice Department in April sentenced two Americans for helping North Koreans obtain remote IT work with US companies and raising more millions of dollars in illicit revenue for its weapons programmes. The ruse "placed North Korean IT workers on the payrolls of unwitting US companies and in US computer systems, thereby potentially harming our national security," said John Eisenberg, assistant attorney general for national security.

items.title: Storm leaves elderly woman's heritage home in ruins in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A heritage house belonging to an elderly woman in Batu 17½, Kampung Melekek here was badly damaged after a storm on Tuesday. Automotive factory worker Asrol Md Yusop, 41, said he was relieved his mother, Zaliha Sabtu, 72, was not at home at the time as she is temporarily staying with relatives elsewhere. He said furniture, electrical items and household belongings were damaged by water.

items.title: German fertiliser makers and farmers struggle with Iran war fallout
items.summary: Wittenberg, GERMANY: As Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz roils the global economy, one German town has been scrambling to help make up the shortfall in essential supplies of fertilisers. DOMINO EFFECT At SKW's sprawling 220-hectare site, a 23-kilometre rail network transports urea, ammonia and finished fertilisers, destined for sites across Germany and elsewhere in Europe. SKW is Germany's largest producer of urea, an essential component of fertilisers.

items.title: Search radius grows to 29km for girl swept away in Bandar Mahkota Cheras drain
items.summary: KAJANG, May 3 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for a nine-year-old girl who is feared to have drowned after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras, entered its third day today with the search being extended to Sungai Langat. Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations commander Izman Adnan said the radius covered a distance of five kilometres (km) from the location of the incident around Pangsapuri Sri Hijau to the end of Sungai Long which connects to Sungai Langat. He said the search today was also carried out from Sungai Langat as far as 24 km towards the Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) water retention pond in Putrajaya, making the total search radius 29 km.

items.title: After controversy in KL, Sarawak rep says Sibu should host ‘Rain Rave’ festivals instead
items.summary: SIBU, May 3 — Bawang Assan assemblyman Datuk Sri Wong Soon Koh has proposed that Sibu host an annual water music festival, saying the district is ready to embrace such initiatives amid controversy surrounding a similar event in Peninsular Malaysia. In a statement, Wong expressed full support for Minister of Tourism, Arts and Culture and Progressive Democratic Party (PDP) president Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing following objections raised against the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in the peninsula. Wong revealed he would formally engage with Tiong to explore the possibility of hosting a similar festival at Sibu Town Square and to propose making it a signature annual event under PDP and Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS), with the aim of positioning Sibu as a tourism and arts hub in central Sarawak.

items.title: Jalan BDC hit-and-run: Kuching mayor appeals for information after cyclist’s death
items.summary: KUCHING, May 3 — The community and netizens are urged to stop speculating on the alleged hit-and-run incident on Friday that killed a 56-year-old cyclist. In making this call, Kuching South mayor Datuk Wee Hong Seng appealed to eye-witnesses to come forward with information to assist the investigation. “While we hope that people could stop speculating and pinning blame, we also urge the culprit to come forward and own up,” he said during his Facebook live session ‘Shall WEE Talk’ on Saturday.

items.title: Father killed, two daughters hurt after trailer crushes family pick-up on LPT2
items.summary: Kemaman police chief Supt Mohd Razi Rosli said the victim, Rosli Yusuf, 55, was pronounced dead at the scene due to severe internal injuries in the incident, which occurred at 6.33 pm. He said the victim’s two daughters, Auni Ukhratan Basyirah, 15, and Iliya Markisa Qurrata Aini, 23, sustained minor injuries and were taken to Kemaman Hospital for treatment. “Initial investigations found that the accident involved a trailer carrying a load of bricks, a Mitsubishi Triton pick-up truck, and a sport utility vehicle (SUV),” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Zahid’s Umno balances solo ambition with alliance flexibility for GE16
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — Barisan Nasional (BN) is “almost certain” to contest the next general election independently but must remain open to political understandings to avoid disastrous three-cornered fights, said chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. “In the coming election, I am almost certain we will go solo,” Zahid said in the interview for Umno’s 80th anniversary. “We must avoid seat overlaps and three-cornered fights.

items.title: Girl, 10, drowns during family picnic at Jelebu’s Taman Eko Rimba Jeram Toi
items.summary: Jelebu district police chief Supt Azizan Said said his department received information about the incident from a 38-year-old local man who is also a member of the Malaysian Civil Defence Force (APM) at 5.44 pm. During the incident, the victim and her mother were believed to have slipped and fell into the pond. “The victim’s mother, however, managed to save herself, while the victim sank to the bottom of the pond.

items.title: George Town hosts grand Vaisakhi open house at Padang Kota Lama
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 3 — The Penang-level Vaisakhi Open House at Jalan Padang Kota Lama drew more than 1,000 visitors from within and outside the state, in a vibrant celebration filled with a spirit of harmony. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the meaningful celebration, now in its 22nd edition in Penang, reflected the enduring presence of the Sikh community in the state as well as a shared commitment to cultural harmony and mutual respect. Chow said Penang’s success has always been driven by its people, who continue to work hand in hand, respect one another and move forward together as one.

items.title: MyDigital ID integration with MyJPJ running smoothly since May 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — The implementation of the MyDigital ID Single Sign-On (SSO) integration for the MyJPJ application, enforced since May 1, has been operating smoothly without any technical disruptions. MyDigital ID said in a statement that the system remained stable and was functioning as planned, enabling users to access services more easily and securely. It said that to date, seven million MyJPJ users had registered and logged in using MyDigital ID, with the total number of MyDigital ID users reaching 12 million.

items.title: Search continues for 9-year-old girl swept away in Cheras drain
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 4 — The search for a girl, nine, who went missing after falling into a drain in Bandar Mahkota Cheras Friday was suspended at 7.30 pm yesterdat and resumed this morning. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said 22 personnel, including seven water rescue team members were involved in yesterday’s operations. The search involved an area of about five-kilometre radius, he added.

items.title: Palm oil tanker flips, spills contents near Bandar Sultan Suleiman in Klang
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 3 — A palm oil tanker overturned and spilled its content on the road heading to Bandar Sultan Suleiman near here yesterday. North Klang district police chief S. Vijaya Rao said at about 1.55 pm the lorry involved, which was from Rantau Panjang, Klang, was going down a bridge when the tank lock came off the lorry’s head, causing the tank to fall into the left lane of the highway exit. Earlier, a video went viral on social media showing an oil tanker on a road in Klang suddenly loosen from lorry and overturned, spilling oil on the road.

items.title: Penang sees first heat stroke death of 2026 as runner collapses near end of 30km race
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 3 — The Penang State Health Department (JKNPP) has announced that a local man has died from heat stroke, which was reported on April 26, after the victim participated in a 30-kilometre running event in the city. JKNPP said the case involving a 42-year-old man was the first case of heat-related illness recorded in Penang in 2026. “The man participated in a 30-kilometre running event in George Town which started at 6.30 am on April 25 and the running route also involved hilly tracks through forest areas around the city and at about 4 pm, the victim was reported to have fainted near the finish line.

items.title: Viral clip captures moments when bystanders subdue armed woman at Rain Rave Music Festival in KL (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 3 — A 28-year-old woman armed with a knife was arrested on the final night of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival in Bukit Bintang yesterday, with a viral video showing members of the public bravely intervening to subdue her before police arrived. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus confirmed the arrest, which occurred amidst a crowd of approximately 30,000 attendees. “Tonight, we have arrested a 28-year-old woman for possession of a weapon,” Fadil told the media after the event.

items.title: Zahid: Political cooperation no longer optional, but Malay interests remain priority
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said today that political cooperation is now essential rather than optional, but stressed that it must be grounded in clear and firm principles. The Federal Constitution must remain the foundation, and the social contract must continue to bind us. “We must approach politics with wisdom and maturity,” he said.

items.title: Khairy: Umno’s image only restored ‘to some extent’, faces uphill battle to regain trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said Umno must acknowledge its past mistakes as a party before it can begin rebuilding trust with voters who have turned away from it. He said the party’s image had improved only “to some extent”. Khairy said Umno must recognise its past failings collectively, while also noting what he described as the party’s wider contributions to the country.

items.title: Khairy: No official Umno role yet, too early to share details
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Khairy Jamaluddin today said he does not currently hold any official position in Umno. He added that it was still too early to disclose details of any responsibilities he may take on. “I have been asked to look into one or two specific matters, but it is still too early to share details as I have not formally begun,” he told reporters when met at the Umno 80th anniversary convention here today.

items.title: Sabah records 357 monkey malaria cases, one death as hotspots emerge
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 2 — Sabah recorded 357 monkey malaria cases and one death in the first four months of this year, with Ranau, Kudat and Tawau as the main hotspots, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. All health clinics and hospitals are capable of detecting these monkey malaria cases,” he told reporters after launching the Wellness Hub in Sandakan today. Monkey malaria, also known as zoonotic malaria or simian malaria, is transmitted from monkeys to humans via mosquito bites and is typically caused by the parasite Plasmodium knowlesi.

items.title: Woman jumps out of moving car in Klang, cites fear of e-hailing driver watching her
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 2 — A 25-year-old e-hailing passenger jumped from a moving car near Taman Eng Ann, Klang, yesterday morning after becoming fearful and anxious during the journey when she believed the driver was watching her. Klang Utara District Police Chief ACP S. Vijaya Rao said in the incident, the local woman claimed the driver had been watching her through the rear-view mirror without any clear reason, before she opened the door on the right side and leapt out. “The car was driven by a 47-year-old local man, a full-time e-hailing driver, who at the time was carrying two local female passengers.

items.title: Johari Ghani: Umno must accept reality of 26 seats, face internal weaknesses
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani today said the party is in a rebuilding phase and must accept its current political reality, including holding only 26 parliamentary seats and no longer being the ruling party. He said Umno’s long history of political dominance and success must now be followed by honest reflection as the party works to regain its strength. Johari said any political recovery must begin with awareness of the party’s current position, warning that no organisation can move forward without first acknowledging reality.

items.title: Health Ministry sets up task force to tackle doctor shortage, focus on Sabah
items.summary: SANDAKAN, May 2 — The Ministry of Health has established an Interministerial Special Task Force to examine the issue of shortages of doctors and medical specialists, including aspects of talent retention in the national healthcare sector. “I am aware of this and have requested the (ministry) secretary-general and Health director-general to work together with the Ministry of Higher Education, as well as the Public Service Department and the Ministry of Finance to address this issue, particularly in Sabah,” he said. On another matter, he said the ministry will soon submit a counter-proposal regarding operational expenditure cuts by the Ministry of Finance involving the healthcare sector.

items.title: Dr Akmal: No compromise on ‘red lines’ as Negeri Sembilan proves Umno won’t bow to pressure
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh said that the party remains grounded in principle, insisting it will not be swayed by political pressure. “I want to make it clear that Umno is a party founded on principles. Umno is not a party that simply follows the prevailing winds.

items.title: Puteri Umno chief urges stronger support for M40 women, says group often overlooked
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 2 — Puteri Umno chief Datuk Nurul Amal Mohd Fauzi today urged the government to strengthen support for young women in the middle-income (M40) group, warning that many remain overlooked despite facing mounting economic and social pressures. “This group is not merely carrying the burden of daily living, but is also balancing responsibilities and expectations,” she said. She also called on the government to move beyond short-term assistance and instead develop a more comprehensive ecosystem that would enable young women to thrive.

items.title: Kota Bharu college offers support to family of teen student murdered in Ketereh, pledges full cooperation in police investigation
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, May 2 — Kolej Poly-Tech Mara Sdn Bhd (KPTMSB) has expressed sorrow over the death of its 19-year-old student from its campus here who was found in Ketereh, Kelantan with multiple stab wounds yesterday. The college said it is fully cooperating with the police amid the murder investigations. Nurfisya Zulkifly was discovered dead by the roadside near a paddy field in Kampung Simah in Ketereh with 61 stab wounds on May 1, 2026, Kelantan police said.
